paper_title: Grain-Size Analysis and Endmember Modeling in Quaternary Oxbow Lake Sedimentation

methods.description: Process for obtaining grain-size distributions (GSDs) of pretreated sediment core samples using laser diffraction.

steps.description: GSDs of the pretreated sediment core samples were obtained at one cm intervals for 42 grain-size classes ranging from 0.1 to 500 µm by laser diffraction using the Easysizer20 laser particle analyzer (OMEC).
steps.searchable_summary: obtain grain-size distributions using laser diffraction

methods.description: Unmixing of measured grain-size distributions into unimodal endmembers using the AnalySize v.1.2.0 EMM algorithm.

steps.description: The unmixing of the measured GSDs was performed using the AnalySize v.1.2.0 EMM algorithm, a freely available MATLAB-based graphic user interface (GUI) software.
steps.searchable_summary: unmix grain-size distributions using AnalySize EMM algorithm

steps.description: The optimum number of the EMs is determined based on the coefficient of determination (R 2 ) and the angular deviation (θ) between the endmembers and the GSDs.
steps.searchable_summary: determine optimum number of endmembers using R2 and angular deviation

steps.description: The parameterization method used is the General Weibull Distribution function.
steps.searchable_summary: apply General Weibull Distribution function for parameterization
